name: Suspicious In-Memory Module Execution Detected
description: Detects suspicious processes accessing other processes' memory space in a manner consistent with reflective loading or other in-memory code execution techniques. This is a strong indicator of an adversary attempting to bypass security defenses by executing malicious code directly in memory.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: a process is attempting to access the memory of another process in a suspicious manner, which is a strong indicator of **In-Memory Module Execution** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1055**). The call trace details are **{call_trace}**, and the access rights are **{access}**. This is often an attempt by an adversary to inject and execute malicious code without dropping a file to disk, which is a common **Defense Evasion** technique. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action and to remove the malicious process. The event occurred under the user **{user}**.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: call_trace
parameters.description: The call trace of the process that accessed the memory.

parameters.name: access
parameters.description: The access rights requested by the process.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the action.
